summary: An oil-rich Arab state devastated by Saddam Hussein's dictatorship, the Iran-Iraq War, two US-led Gulf Wars, ISIS occupation, and chronic sectarian conflict — now a weak democracy caught between US and Iranian influence.

history.detail: UK draws borders combining Sunni, Shia, Kurdish populations with no historical basis.
Military coup; King Faisal II killed; Republic proclaimed.
Saddam invades Iran; 8-year war with ~1M dead; US backs Saddam.
Saddam's forces kill 5,000 Kurds with chemical weapons. CONFIRMED.
Annexation triggers Gulf War; US-led coalition expels Iraq 1991.
WMD claims proven false; Saddam captured; sectarian civil war erupts.
100,000+ killed in sectarian violence; Al-Qaeda in Iraq rises.
Islamic State captures 1/3 of Iraq; Yazidi genocide; US returns with airstrikes.
Mosul liberated; ISIS territorial caliphate destroyed.

resources.oil_gas: 5th largest oil reserves globally; OPEC member
resources.water: Tigris and Euphrates — cradle of civilisation; rivers threatened by Turkish dams and climate change
resources.agriculture: Dates; ancient Mesopotamian farmland degraded by war and water stress

follow_the_money.detail: Billions in US contracts post-2003 to Halliburton, KBR etc; massive corruption

theories_disputed.detail: WMDs did not exist. Intelligence was manipulated or fabricated to justify 2003 invasion. Chilcot Inquiry and Senate Intelligence Committee confirmed.

connections.detail: US troops remain; ongoing security cooperation
Shia-majority government with deep Iranian influence; Iranian-backed militias
Sunni-Shia proxy dimension; Saudi relations improving
